questions.stem: お<ruby>客様<rt>きゃくさま</rt></ruby>＿＿＿、<ruby>失礼<rt>しつれい</rt></ruby>な<ruby>態度<rt>たいど</rt></ruby>を<ruby>取<rt>と</rt></ruby>ってはいけない。
questions.why: に対して marks the TARGET an attitude or action is directed at. にとって would mark whose viewpoint something is judged from.

questions.stem: <ruby>兄<rt>あに</rt></ruby>は<ruby>静<rt>しず</rt></ruby>かなの＿＿＿、<ruby>弟<rt>おとうと</rt></ruby>はとてもにぎやかだ。
questions.why: The contrastive use: A is quiet, WHEREAS B is lively. Note the の that a preceding clause needs before に対して.

questions.stem: その<ruby>質問<rt>しつもん</rt></ruby>＿＿＿、<ruby>彼<rt>かれ</rt></ruby>は<ruby>何<rt>なに</rt></ruby>も<ruby>答<rt>こた</rt></ruby>えなかった。
questions.why: The question is what his (non-)response was directed at. について would mean he said nothing ABOUT the topic.
